name: Adobe Acrobat Sign API
what_it_does: Enterprise-grade e-signature platform API for sending, tracking, and managing legally binding digital signature workflows at scale, with deep integration into Adobe Document Cloud.
use_cases: Sending contracts and agreements for electronic signature from automated workflows
Building custom signature flows with bulk send capabilities
Integrating e-signature into enterprise CRM or ERP systems
Tracking agreement status (sent, viewed, signed, completed) programmatically
Managing signature delegation, reminders, and expiration workflows
not_for: Simple PDF generation without signing requirements
Startups or SMBs (Adobe Sign is expensive for low volume)
Consumer-facing signature UX (DocuSign and PandaDoc have better consumer UX)
best_when: You're in an enterprise environment with existing Adobe Document Cloud licensing and need compliant e-signature workflows with strict legal requirements.
avoid_when: You need a cost-effective signature solution for low-to-medium volume — PandaDoc or HelloSign/Dropbox Sign are more accessible.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with access tokens and refresh tokens. Scoped permissions (agreement_read, agreement_send, user_read, etc.). Service accounts use OAuth2 client credentials flow for server-to-server.

pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing negotiated directly with Adobe. Often bundled with Acrobat Pro or Document Cloud enterprise licenses. API access requires appropriate plan.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by account tier; typically 5-50 requests/second

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses include code and message. Some error codes are Adobe-internal and require documentation to decode. Developer docs can be outdated.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Sending an agreement twice sends two separate agreements. Agents must track sent agreement IDs to avoid duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Adobe Sign API has multiple datacenter regions (NA, EU, APAC) — API endpoint must match account region
Access tokens expire after 3600 seconds — agents must implement token refresh with refresh_token
Transient document upload is required before creating agreements — two-step process
Agreement status polling required — webhook setup is complex but preferable for agents
Library templates vs ad-hoc agreements have different API flows
OAuth app requires approval for production use — sandbox and production use different client IDs
